lead: A war-driven spike in gasoline and diesel sent U.S. inflation to its biggest monthly jump in nearly four years, adding fresh pressure to consumers, markets, and Washington. In tech, AI safety fears intensified around Anthropic's new model, security concerns hit OpenAI after an attack on Sam Altman's home, and industry attention stayed on Xbox management criticism and Apple's foldable iPhone plans.
summary_points: March inflation accelerated sharply as fuel costs surged after the Iran war, complicating the economic and political outlook.
Anthropic's new Mythos model drew cybersecurity concern, highlighting rising anxiety over advanced AI capabilities and misuse risks.
A suspect was arrested after allegedly attacking Sam Altman's home and threatening OpenAI headquarters in San Francisco.
Former Bethesda executive criticism renewed scrutiny of Xbox stewardship, while Apple's foldable iPhone rumors advanced toward preproduction.
